books.title: OSINT Techniques: Resources for Uncovering Online Information
books.author: Michael Bazzell
books.description: The definitive guide to OSINT investigation techniques. Covers search engines, social networks, documents, images, and more.

books.title: Extreme Privacy: What It Takes to Disappear
books.author: Michael Bazzell
books.description: Comprehensive guide to digital privacy and disappearing from online databases. Practical steps for protecting your identity.

books.title: Hacking: The Art of Exploitation
books.author: Jon Erickson
books.description: Foundational text covering programming, networking, shellcode, cryptology, and exploitation from the ground up. Second edition.

books.title: RTFM: Red Team Field Manual Book 1
books.author: Ben Clark
books.description: Quick-reference guide for red team operators. Covers Linux, Windows, networking, and common attack commands.

books.title: RTFM: Red Team Field Manual Book 2
books.author: Ben Clark & Nick Downer
books.description: Updated and expanded red team reference. New techniques, commands, and procedures for modern red team operations.

books.title: BTFM: Blue Team Field Manual Book 1
books.author: Ben Clark & Alan J. White
books.description: Quick-reference guide for defensive security. Incident response, forensics, and network defense commands.

books.title: FTFM: Forensic Team Field Manual Book 2
books.author: Alan J. White & Ben Clark
books.description: Field manual for digital forensics teams. Quick-reference commands and procedures for forensic investigations.

books.title: The Web Application Hacker's Handbook: First Edition
books.author: Marcus Pinto & Dafydd Stuttard
books.description: The original guide to finding and exploiting web application security flaws. Written by the creator of Burp Suite.

books.title: The Web Application Hacker's Handbook: Second Edition
books.author: Dafydd Stuttard & Marcus Pinto
books.description: Comprehensive updated guide to discovering and exploiting security flaws in web applications. By the creator of Burp Suite.

books.title: Penetration Testing: A Hands-On Introduction to Hacking
books.author: Georgia Weidman
books.description: Hands-on introduction to pentesting covering Kali Linux, Metasploit, social engineering, and reporting.

books.title: Network Security Assessment: Know Your Network
books.author: Chris McNab
books.description: Practical guide to identifying and assessing network security risks using real-world attack techniques. Third edition.

books.title: Practical Malware Analysis: The Hands-On Guide to Dissecting Malicious Software
books.author: Michael Sikorski & Andrew Honig
books.description: The hands-on guide to dissecting malware. Covers static analysis, dynamic analysis, and reverse engineering.

books.title: The Art of Deception: Controlling the Human Element of Security
books.author: Kevin D. Mitnick & William L. Simon
books.description: Mitnick reveals how social engineering is the greatest threat to security. Real-world stories and defenses against manipulation.

books.title: The Art of Intrusion: The Real Stories Behind the Exploits of Hackers, Intruders and Deceivers
books.author: Kevin D. Mitnick & William L. Simon
books.description: Real-world stories of hackers at work. Each chapter covers a different attack vector with lessons learned.

books.title: The Art of Invisibility: The World's Most Famous Hacker Teaches You How to Be Safe in the Age of Big Brother and Big Data
books.author: Kevin D. Mitnick & Mikko Hypponen
books.description: Mitnick's guide to online privacy and anonymity. Practical techniques for protecting yourself from surveillance and data collection.

books.title: Mastering Blockchain: First Edition
books.author: Imran Bashir
books.description: Deeper insights into decentralization, cryptography, Bitcoin, and popular blockchain frameworks.

books.title: Mastering Blockchain: Second Edition
books.author: Imran Bashir
books.description: Distributed ledger technology, decentralization, and smart contracts explained.

books.title: Mastering Blockchain: Third Edition
books.author: Imran Bashir
books.description: Deep dive into distributed ledgers, consensus protocols, smart contracts, DApps, cryptocurrencies, Ethereum, and more.

books.title: Mastering Blockchain: Fourth Edition
books.author: Imran Bashir
books.description: Inner workings of blockchain, from cryptography and decentralized identities, to DeFi, NFTs and Web3.

books.title: Investigating Cryptocurrencies: Understanding, Extracting, and Analyzing Blockchain Evidence
books.author: Nick Furneaux
books.description: Law enforcement and forensic guide to tracing cryptocurrency transactions and identifying wallet owners.

books.title: Black Hat Python: First Edition
books.author: Justin Seitz
books.description: Python programming for hackers and pentesters. Network sniffing, web attacks, and trojan development.

books.title: Black Hat Python: Second Edition
books.author: Justin Seitz & Tom Arnold
books.description: Updated Python programming for hackers and pentesters. Modern techniques with Python 3.

books.title: Gray Hat Python: Python Programming for Hackers and Reverse Engineers
books.author: Justin Seitz
books.description: Python for reverse engineering and security research. Debuggers, fuzzers, code injection, and more.

hardware.title: HackRF One
hardware.description: Open-source software-defined radio platform. Transmit and receive 1 MHz to 6 GHz. Essential for RF security research.

hardware.title: Flipper Zero
hardware.description: Multi-tool for pentesters and hardware hackers. RFID, NFC, infrared, sub-GHz radio, GPIO, and BadUSB in one device.

hardware.title: Alfa AWUS036ACH
hardware.description: Dual-band AC1200 USB WiFi adapter with monitor mode and packet injection support. The go-to for wireless auditing.

hardware.title: Proxmark3 RDV4
hardware.description: Advanced RFID/NFC research tool. Read, write, clone, and emulate HF and LF proximity cards.

hardware.title: RTL-SDR V4 Kit
hardware.description: Budget software-defined radio receiver kit. 500 kHz to 1.75 GHz coverage. Includes dipole antenna kit.

hardware.title: Apple MacBook Pro with Touch Bar (Late 2019)
hardware.description: 16" Intel i9 with 32GB RAM and 2TB SSD. Space Grey. The last Intel-based MacBook Pro — still a powerhouse for virtualization and running multiple VMs.

hardware.title: Apple MacBook Air (Late 2020)
hardware.description: 13.3" M1 Silicon with 16GB RAM and 2TB SSD. Space Grey. Fanless design with all-day battery — perfect portable OSINT workstation.

hardware.title: Apple MacBook Pro (Late 2021)
hardware.description: 14.2" M1 Silicon with 16GB RAM and 1TB SSD. Space Grey. ProMotion display with the first Apple Silicon pro chip.

hardware.title: Apple MacBook Pro (2022)
hardware.description: 13.3" M2 Silicon with 24GB RAM and 1TB SSD. Space Grey. Compact form factor with upgraded unified memory for heavy workloads.

hardware.title: Apple MacBook Air (2024)
hardware.description: 13.6" M3 Silicon with 8GB RAM and 512GB SSD. Midnight. Lightweight everyday machine with the latest Apple Silicon.

hardware.title: Apple MacBook Pro (2026)
hardware.description: 16.2" M5 Max with 48GB RAM and 2TB SSD. Silver. Top-tier performance for running full lab environments, large-scale analysis, and parallel tooling.

hardware.title: LG Gram Pro (2025)
hardware.description: 17" Intel Core Ultra 7 with 32GB RAM and 2TB SSD. Obsidian Black. Ultra-lightweight 17-inch — ideal for extended fieldwork with a large display.

hardware.title: LG Gram Business with Touchscreen (2025)
hardware.description: 17" Intel Core Ultra 9 with 32GB RAM and 4TB SSD. Black. Touchscreen business-class with massive storage for forensic image handling.

hardware.title: ASUS Zenbook with Touchscreen (2026)
hardware.description: 14" Intel Core Ultra 9 with 32GB RAM and 4TB SSD. Black. Compact touchscreen ultrabook with top-end specs for portable security work.

hardware.title: ASUS Vivobook (2025)
hardware.description: 18" AMD Ryzen 7 with 32GB RAM and 1TB SSD. Quiet Blue. Large-screen budget workhorse for lab setups and multi-window recon workflows.
